apple_integration_footer: Enable Apple Calendar tools for this persona by default. Requires a connected Apple Calendar account.
notion_integration_footer: Enable Notion tools for this persona by default. Requires a connected Notion account.

recurrence_footer_weekly: Runs weekly. Time is shown in your local timezone but stored as UTC.

skill_runtime_workspace_desc: Full workspace environment for code execution and analysis

home_hero2_desc: Connect your own OpenRouter account, pick the models you want, and pay only for what you use. No platform markup. No monthly subscription for model access.
home_hiw_desc: Most AI apps charge a monthly fee and limit which models you can use. Edge works differently — you connect to OpenRouter, choose from over 150 models, and only pay for the conversations you have.
home_hiw_step1_body: Create a free account in seconds. Then link your OpenRouter account — Edge walks you through it step by step. OpenRouter is where the AI models live, and connecting takes about a minute.
home_hiw_step2_body: Pick from over 150 AI models — including GPT, Claude, Gemini, and many more. Not sure which to choose? Edge shows you what each model is good at, and you can switch anytime, even mid-conversation.
home_hiw_step3_title: Only pay for what you use
home_hiw_step3_body: No monthly subscription for AI access. You add credit to your OpenRouter account and only spend it when you chat. Most conversations cost a fraction of a penny. Your balance is always visible so there are no surprises.
home_cap_desc: Chat is the starting point, not the ceiling. Personas, memory, search, files, scheduled jobs, autonomous sessions, and a spatial canvas turn Edge into a workspace you actually stay in.
home_cap_chat_body: Add up to three models to one conversation. Compare answers side by side, fork a message to explore a different direction, or regenerate with a different model. Queue follow-ups while the AI is still responding.
home_cap_personas_body: Create AI identities with their own name, avatar, system prompt, model, temperature, and tools. Assign personas to chats, scheduled jobs, or autonomous sessions. Set a default persona so every new conversation starts the way you want.
home_cap_memory_body: Edge can remember facts across conversations — your preferences, projects, writing style, relationships, and goals. Memory is categorised, searchable, and fully visible. Set it to save automatically, ask first, or stay off entirely.
home_cap_search_body: Internet search is available on the free tier. Pro unlocks deeper modes: web search at three complexity levels, and a full research pipeline that plans queries, iterates on sources, and synthesises a cited paper. Attach results to any conversation.
home_cap_jobs_body: Set recurring AI tasks — daily briefings, weekly reports, monitoring jobs — and let them run in the background. Each job can have up to five steps with their own model, persona, search mode, and integrations. Results appear as regular conversations.
home_cap_auto_body: Let multiple personas debate, brainstorm, or review each other's work without your input. Configure the number of cycles, set a moderator, enable stop-on-consensus, and intervene at any point. Cost estimates shown before you start.
home_cap_files_body: Attach images, PDFs, and Office documents to any chat. Pro adds a persistent knowledge base — upload reference material once and pull it into conversations or scheduled jobs whenever you need it. Edge can also generate documents, spreadsheets, and presentations.
home_cap_ideascapes_body: Switch any conversation into a spatial canvas. Arrange AI responses as movable nodes, draw non-linear paths between ideas, and zoom across the full map. Positions persist — come back to the same layout later.
home_int_desc: Connect your existing accounts and give Edge access to the services you already depend on. Every integration works inside regular chats, persona conversations, and scheduled jobs.
home_int_google_body: Read, search, and send emails. Upload and browse Drive files. View and create calendar events. All accessible from chat, personas, and scheduled jobs.
home_int_google_coming_soon_body: Google Workspace is coming soon on web while Google scope approval is pending.
home_int_ms_body: Same capabilities as Google — email, file storage, and calendar management — for Microsoft accounts. Manage folders, move messages, create events.
home_int_notion_body: Search your workspace, read and create pages, query and update databases. Use Notion as a living knowledge source inside any conversation.
home_int_apple_body: Native access to your Apple Calendar through system permissions. List, create, update, and delete events without leaving the chat.
home_int_footer: All integrations are Pro features. Each connection is secured through industry-standard authentication.
home_int_google_coming_soon_note: Google Workspace is coming soon on the public web app. Microsoft 365, Notion, and Apple Calendar are available now.
home_pricing_desc: You already pay per-token through OpenRouter. Edge doesn't add a second subscription on top. The free tier is a fully functional AI chat app. Pro is a single one-time purchase that unlocks every advanced feature forever.
home_pricing_free_desc: A complete multi-model AI chat with basic search, file attachments, folders, and full settings. Not a crippled demo — a real tool.
home_pricing_pro_desc: Everything in Free, plus the full toolkit — personas, memory, autonomous sessions, advanced search, scheduled jobs, integrations, knowledge base, ideascapes, document generation, and more.
home_free_1: Single and multi-model chat (up to 3 models)
home_free_2: 150+ models — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Mistral, and more
home_free_3: Basic web search in any conversation
home_free_6: Folders, bulk operations, and chat search
home_free_7: Per-chat model, temperature, and token overrides
home_free_8: Full model catalog with capability filters
home_free_10: OpenRouter balance monitoring and low-credit alerts
home_pro_1: Custom AI personas with system prompts, models, and tools
home_pro_2: Persistent cross-conversation memory (auto, ask-first, or off)
home_pro_3: Autonomous multi-persona sessions with moderator and consensus detection
home_pro_4: Ideascapes — spatial canvas for non-linear conversations
home_pro_5: Web search at three complexity levels (quick, thorough, comprehensive)
home_pro_6: Research pipeline — plan, search, iterate, and synthesise cited papers
home_pro_7: Scheduled jobs with multi-step pipelines and recurrence
home_pro_8: Knowledge base — upload once, reference from any chat or job
home_pro_9: Document generation (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, CSV, Markdown)
home_pro_10: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Notion, and Apple Calendar integrations
home_pro_11: Parallel subagents — split work across up to 3 focused helpers
home_pro_12: AI skills — attach specialised behaviours to personas
home_byok_body1: Edge uses a bring-your-own-key model. You connect your OpenRouter account and every token is billed at OpenRouter's listed rate — no markup from us. A typical GPT-4o conversation costs fractions of a cent. Smaller models like GPT-4o Mini or Gemini Flash are even cheaper. Some models are completely free.
home_byok_body2: Your OpenRouter credit balance is always visible in the app. Edge sends you a notification when credits run low so you never hit a wall mid-conversation.
home_cta_desc: Multi-model chat, basic search, file attachments, and folders — all free, no time limit. When you want personas, memory, automations, and the rest, Pro is a one-time unlock.
priv_seo_desc: How NanthAI Edge collects, uses, stores, and deletes account data, chat content, memories, files, and connected integration data.
priv_hero_desc: NanthAI Edge is a native mobile app backed by cloud services that store chats, personas, scheduled jobs, connected accounts, and other data needed to make the product work. This policy explains what we collect, why we collect it, when provider data is used, how it is stored, and how you can request deletion or support.
priv_s1_body: This Privacy Policy applies to the NanthAI Edge mobile apps, related support operations, and the backend services used to operate Edge. It covers information we collect directly from you, information generated as you use the app, and information we receive when you connect external providers such as Google, Microsoft, Notion, or Apple Calendar.
priv_s2a_body: When you sign in, we use Clerk for authentication. This may include identifiers such as your email address, account ID, session metadata, and basic profile information required to keep your account signed in and secure.
priv_s2b_title: App content you create or store
priv_s2b_item1: Chats, messages, and related conversation metadata.
priv_s2b_item2: Personas, persona images, persona settings, and avatar assets.
priv_s2b_item3: Memories and memory embeddings when memory is enabled for your account.
priv_s2b_item4: Uploaded files, generated files, and file attachment metadata.
priv_s2b_item5: Scheduled jobs, recurring prompts, execution history, and job settings.
priv_s2b_item6: Model preferences, tool settings, and integration preferences.
priv_s2c_body1: If you connect third-party services, we store the minimum tokens, credentials, or connection metadata required to use those integrations on your behalf. Depending on the provider, this may include access tokens, refresh tokens, expiry times, scopes, account email addresses, workspace identifiers, or connection status.
priv_s2c_body2: For Apple Calendar, the current connection flow uses your Apple Account email plus an Apple app-specific password for iCloud Calendar access. Those credentials are stored server-side so Edge can perform calendar actions for you until you disconnect the integration or revoke the password in your Apple Account settings.
priv_s2d_body: We may collect device tokens for notifications, app diagnostics, error logs, feature usage signals, and basic technical metadata needed to keep the service stable, investigate problems, and improve the product.
priv_s3_item1: To authenticate you and keep your account secure.
priv_s3_item2: To deliver chat, persona, memory, file, and scheduled-task features.
priv_s3_item3: To connect to external providers you explicitly authorize.
priv_s3_item4: To run requested or scheduled user-facing actions across connected tools.
priv_s3_item5: To troubleshoot bugs, provide support, and improve product quality.
priv_s3_item6: To enforce product limits, entitlements, and anti-abuse protections.
priv_s4_body: NanthAI Edge relies on third-party services to operate. These currently include services such as Clerk for identity, Convex for backend data and functions, OpenRouter for model access when you connect it, Apple and Google for app store billing and platform services, and provider APIs such as Google, Microsoft, Notion, or iCloud when you choose to connect them. Those providers process data under their own terms and privacy policies.
priv_s5_title: 5. How connected provider data is used
priv_s5_body1: Connected provider data is only accessed when you explicitly connect that provider and then use a related feature, such as a chat tool call, a scheduled job, or a provider-specific action. We do not connect providers silently in the background.
priv_s5_body2: When you ask Edge to use a connected provider, the provider response may be read by the app, stored in backend records needed to complete the request, and included in the context sent to the AI model you selected through OpenRouter or another model provider. This happens only to deliver the feature you requested, such as summarising an email, drafting a reply, creating a calendar event, or updating a Notion page.
priv_s5a_body1: If you connect Google, Edge may access Gmail, Google Drive, and Google Calendar data that is necessary for the tools you enable. This may include email message content and metadata, file metadata and file contents, and calendar event data.
priv_s5a_body2: Google Workspace data is used only to provide or improve user-facing features that you request inside Edge. We do not sell Google user data and do not use it for advertising. We do not let humans read Google Workspace data except when reasonably necessary for security, support, legal compliance, or with your explicit request or consent.
priv_s5b_body: If you connect Microsoft 365, Edge may access Outlook mail data, OneDrive file data, and Microsoft calendar data that is necessary for the tools you enable. This data is used only to execute the actions you request through chats or scheduled jobs.
priv_s5c_body: If you connect Notion, Edge may access pages, blocks, databases, and workspace metadata that you explicitly authorize through Notion's connection flow. This data is used only to provide the Notion features you trigger in the app.
priv_s5d_body: If you connect Apple Calendar, Edge may access iCloud Calendar metadata and event content needed to list, create, update, or delete events on your behalf. The Apple Calendar connection is performed using your Apple Account email and an Apple app-specific password that you provide.
priv_s6_body: Purchases and refunds for paid features are handled through the platform stores (Apple App Store and Google Play). NanthAI Edge stores entitlement state needed to unlock or remove paid functionality. Certain paid features, including memory, may be disabled when Pro access is revoked. If Pro access is revoked or refunded, Edge may delete memory data associated with that paid feature in accordance with the product design.
priv_s7_body1: We retain data for as long as your account or product usage requires it, unless we need to keep it longer for security, legal, or operational reasons.
priv_s7_item1: Chats, personas, files, and settings are retained until you delete them or delete your account.
priv_s7_item2: Connected-provider tokens or credentials are retained until you disconnect the provider, revoke access, or delete your account.
priv_s7_item3: Apple Calendar credentials are retained until you disconnect Apple Calendar, delete your account, or revoke the app-specific password.
priv_s7_item4: Memory data is retained until deleted, your account is deleted, or Pro revocation triggers a memory purge.
priv_s7_item5: Some operational records, such as stale search or job-run records, may be retained only for limited periods for reliability and debugging.
priv_s7_body2: You can delete some data directly in the app. Edge also includes an in-app account deletion flow. If you want your account or related app data deleted, contact support@nanthai.tech.
priv_s8_body: We use commercially reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect data, including secure authentication, backend access controls, and platform storage mechanisms. No internet or cloud system is perfectly secure, so we cannot guarantee absolute security.
priv_s9_item1: You can choose whether to connect third-party services.
priv_s9_item2: You can remove connected integrations inside the app.
priv_s9_item3: You can disable optional features such as memory where supported.
priv_s9_item4: You can request deletion of your account or stored data.
priv_s9_item5: You can revoke provider access from the provider side, such as your Google, Microsoft, Notion, or Apple account settings.
priv_s9_body: For Apple Calendar specifically, you can revoke access by deleting the app-specific password in your Apple Account settings. For Google, Microsoft, and Notion, you can revoke the integration in the relevant provider's account permissions or app management settings.
priv_s10_body1: We do not sell your personal data. We do not use connected-provider data for advertising. Connected-provider data is processed only to deliver the user-facing features you request. If such data is sent to an AI model provider you selected through OpenRouter or another model access layer, that provider's handling is governed by its own terms, privacy policy, and account settings.
priv_s10_body2: The use of information received from Google Workspace APIs will adhere to the Google User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.
priv_s11_body: NanthAI Edge is not directed to children under 16, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 16.
priv_s12_body: We may update this policy as the product evolves. When we make material changes, we will update the date at the top of this page and may provide additional notice in the app or through support channels when appropriate.
priv_s13_body: Privacy questions, deletion requests, or support issues can be sent to
sp_seo_desc: How to contact NanthAI Edge for support, privacy requests, integration issues, and account help.
sp_hero_desc: If you need help with NanthAI Edge, email us directly. This page is also the public support destination used for provider verification and app-store-facing product support.
sp_contact_desc: Use this address for product questions, legal requests, integrations, and account issues. This is the public support contact for NanthAI Edge.
sp_include_1: Your account email or the sign-in method you used.
sp_include_2: A short description of the issue or request.
sp_include_3: Any error message, screenshot, or provider name involved.
sp_include_4: The device, OS version, and app build if you are reporting a bug.
tos_seo_desc: Terms governing the use of the NanthAI Edge mobile app, connected provider tools, and purchases.
tos_hero_desc: These Terms govern your use of NanthAI Edge, including the mobile apps, related support surfaces, and connected service features. By using the app, you agree to these Terms.
tos_s1_body: NanthAI Edge is a native mobile AI application that provides chat, personas, optional memory, scheduled jobs, file handling, and connected tools. Some capabilities depend on third-party services that you choose to connect, including OpenRouter and external providers such as Google, Microsoft, Notion, or Apple Calendar.
tos_s2_body: You must be legally able to use the app and create an account in your jurisdiction. You are responsible for your account activity and for keeping your sign-in credentials secure.
tos_s3_body1: You retain rights to content you submit to NanthAI Edge, such as prompts, uploaded files, persona definitions, and settings. You grant NanthAI the limited rights necessary to host, process, and transmit that content so the app can function.
tos_s3_body2: If you connect third-party providers, you authorize NanthAI Edge to access and act on the data and resources necessary for the features you enable. You are responsible for ensuring that you have permission to connect those accounts and use their content.
tos_s4_body: Edge may rely on third-party model or integration providers. Your use of those providers may also be governed by their own terms and policies. For example, model access through OpenRouter and connected-provider actions through Google, Microsoft, Notion, or Apple infrastructure remain subject to the applicable provider rules.
tos_s5_body1: Paid features are sold through platform stores (Apple App Store and Google Play). Billing, refunds, and restore flows are handled through each platform's systems and policies. NanthAI Edge may enable or disable premium functionality based on the current entitlement state we receive from the platform.
tos_s5_body2: Some features are intentionally Pro-gated. If a purchase is revoked or refunded, access to those features may be removed and related data, such as premium memory data, may be deleted in line with the product design.
tos_s6_item1: Do not use Edge for unlawful, abusive, or fraudulent activity.
tos_s6_item2: Do not attempt to break, scrape, reverse engineer, or interfere with the service.
tos_s6_item3: Do not use connected integrations in ways that violate provider rules or third-party rights.
tos_s6_item4: Do not upload or generate content you do not have the right to use.
tos_s7_body: AI-generated output can be wrong, incomplete, stale, or inappropriate. You are responsible for reviewing outputs and deciding whether to rely on them. Edge is a tool, not a guarantee of accuracy or professional advice.
tos_s8_body: Features may change, be added, or be removed over time. We may update the app, adjust access, or change how specific integrations work as part of product development.
tos_s9_body: We may suspend or terminate access if you violate these Terms, misuse the service, or create risk for NanthAI, connected providers, or other users. You may stop using the app at any time and can request account deletion through support.
tos_s10_body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, NanthAI Edge is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee uninterrupted availability, error-free performance, or that provider integrations will remain available at all times.

fi_seo_desc: Every NanthAI Edge feature explained — multi-model chat, personas, memories, search, scheduled tasks, integrations, and more.
fi_hero_desc: Explore the full set of features — from free essentials to Pro-only tools that turn Edge into a complete AI workspace.
edge_cta_start_free_upgrade: Start free, upgrade when you're ready.
edge_cta_basic_free_pro_power: The basics are free. The full power is one upgrade away.
edge_cta_create_free_account: Create a free account and start chatting.
mmc_seo_desc: Send one prompt to up to three AI models simultaneously. Compare responses side by side, fork the best answer into its own branch, and save your favourite model combos for instant access.
mmc_step1_desc: Tap the + button in the chat input bar and choose Participants. Browse 150+ models with filters, sort options, and a "Help me choose" wizard that recommends models based on your task.
mmc_step2_desc: Type your question or instruction once. Edge sends it to all selected models simultaneously and streams their responses in real time.
mmc_step3_desc: Responses appear vertically stacked in a shared card — each with its own avatar, model label, and action buttons. Scroll through to compare reasoning, style, and accuracy.
mmc_step4_desc: Found the best answer? Fork that response into its own conversation branch and keep chatting with just that model. Your other branches are always one tap away.
mmc_cap_compare_title: Compare up to 3 models at once
mmc_cap_compare_desc: Run the same prompt against different models simultaneously. See how each one interprets your question.
mmc_cap_fork_desc: Pick the best response and fork it into a standalone conversation. Navigate between branches with the branch pill.
mmc_cap_queue_desc: Don't wait for models to finish. Type your next message and it'll be sent as soon as all responses are complete.
mmc_cap_fav_desc: Pin your go-to model combinations to the favorites strip for one-tap access on any new conversation.
mmc_cap_retry_desc: Not happy with one response? Tap retry-different to swap that slot to a new model without losing the rest.
mmc_cap_mix_desc: Combine any model with a persona's system prompt. Compare how the same persona performs on different models.
mmc_cap_compose_title: Composes with search, personas, and more
mmc_cap_compose_desc: Multi-model works with web search, personas, memories, and knowledge base attachments — each participant resolves its own context independently.
mmc_scenario_1: Comparing how different models write code — testing the same algorithm prompt on multiple models to see which produces the cleanest implementation.
mmc_scenario_2: Getting a second opinion on important decisions — asking multiple models to evaluate a business plan, legal question, or technical architecture.
mmc_scenario_3: Finding the best tone for writing — seeing how different models handle creative writing, emails, or marketing copy side by side.
mmc_scenario_4: Learning and research — comparing explanations of complex topics to find the clearest, most accurate answer.
mmc_scenario_5: Testing prompt engineering — seeing how your carefully crafted prompt performs across different model architectures.
po_seo_desc: Browse 150+ AI models with smart filters, 9 sort criteria, and a guided wizard. Filter by free, vision, image gen, and tools. The participant picker helps you find the right model for any task.
po_step1_desc: Tap the + button in the chat input bar and choose Participants. You'll see a searchable list of every model available through OpenRouter — over 300 in total.
po_step2_desc: Narrow the list with capability filters — Free, Vision, Image Gen, and Tools. Sort by 9 criteria including Recommended, Coding, Research, Speed, Value, and Price.
po_step3_title: Use the wizard if you're unsure
po_step3_desc: Tap "Help me choose" to start a 3-step guided wizard. Tell it what you need (coding, research, writing) and what matters most (quality, speed, or value), and it'll recommend the top 3 models.
po_step4_desc: Tap to add models or personas as participants. Select up to 3 for multi-model comparison, or just one for a focused conversation. Your selection is saved per chat.
po_cap_search_desc: Type to search by model name, ID, or provider. Personas appear alongside models so you can mix and match.
po_cap_filters_desc: Filter by Free, No Free (exclude free models), Vision (image input), Image Gen (image output), and Tools (function calling support).
po_cap_sort_desc: Sort by Recommended, Coding, Research, Speed, Value, Image Quality, Price, Context length, or Top This Week. On iOS, you can set a primary and secondary sort.
po_cap_wizard_desc: A 3-step guided flow: pick your task, choose your priority, and see the top 3 models ranked with scores from independent benchmarks.
po_cap_badges_desc: Every model shows badges for its capabilities — vision, tools, context window size, and more. Tap the badge help button to learn what each one means.
po_cap_personas_desc: Personas appear in the picker alongside bare models. Add a persona and its locked model, system prompt, and personality are applied automatically.
po_cap_free_desc: Use the Free filter to find models that cost nothing to run. Great for experimenting without spending credits.
po_cap_persisted_desc: Your filter and sort settings are saved across sessions. The picker remembers how you like to browse so you don't reconfigure it every time.
po_scenario_1: Choosing the right model for a coding task — filter for Tools support, sort by Coding score, and pick the top-ranked model in seconds.
po_scenario_2: Discovering free models for casual chat — toggle the Free filter and browse without worrying about credit spend.
po_scenario_3: Setting up a multi-model comparison — select 3 models with different strengths (speed vs quality vs value) and let them compete on the same prompt.
po_scenario_4: Finding a vision model for image analysis — filter for Vision capability, sort by Recommended, and see which models handle images best.
po_scenario_5: Getting personalised recommendations — use the wizard when you're new to AI models and need guidance on what to try first.
br_seo_desc: Every conversation is a tree. Regenerate responses, retry with different models, or use multi-model chat to create branches. Navigate with branch pills in chat view, or see the full tree in Ideascape.
br_step1_title: Every message is a tree node
br_step1_desc: Under the hood, each message tracks its parent messages. When multiple messages share the same parent, that's a fork. When a message has multiple parents, that's a merge. This tree structure powers all branching behaviour.
br_step2_desc: Branches happen automatically: regenerate a response to create a sibling branch, retry with a different model, or use multi-model chat where each model's reply is a separate branch from the same prompt.
br_step3_desc: At every fork point in the chat, a branch indicator pill appears between messages. It shows which branch you're on (e.g. "Branch 1 of 3") and lets you switch to any sibling branch with a tap.
br_step4_desc: Switch to Ideascape view and the full branch tree becomes a spatial map. Each branch is a visible path. Select nodes as context, then send a new message to create a branch that merges ideas from multiple threads.
br_cap_autodetect_desc: Whenever multiple replies exist for the same message, the app detects the fork and shows branch indicators. No manual setup required.
br_cap_regen_desc: Regenerating a response creates a new sibling branch rather than replacing the original. Both versions are preserved — switch between them at any time.
br_cap_multimodel_desc: In multi-model chat, each model's response is a separate branch from your prompt. Compare them in chat view with branch pills, or spread them out spatially in Ideascape.
br_cap_pills_desc: Interactive pills appear at fork points in the chat timeline. They show your current branch, the total count, and let you navigate to any sibling with a single tap.
br_cap_merges_desc: In Ideascape, select multiple nodes from different branches as context (+ button or Shift+Click), then send a message. The AI responds drawing on all selected context — effectively merging branches.
br_cap_focus_desc: Click a node in Ideascape to set it as the active branch focus. The chat view updates to show that branch's linear history. You can jump between branches without losing your place.
br_cap_tree_desc: Ideascape shows the entire conversation tree at once — forks, parallel threads, and merges are all visible as connected nodes. Zoom out to see the big picture.
br_cap_continue_desc: Navigate to any branch and keep chatting. Each branch maintains its own thread of conversation independently. Your other branches remain untouched.
br_scenario_1: Comparing approaches — asking a question, then regenerating with different models to get multiple perspectives. Switch between branches to evaluate each response.
br_scenario_2: Exploring what-ifs — forking a conversation to try different directions without losing your original thread. Come back to any branch later.
br_scenario_3: Multi-model comparisons — sending one prompt to three models and reviewing each branch side by side in the chat, or spatially in Ideascape.
br_scenario_4: Merging the best ideas — selecting the strongest points from different branches in Ideascape, then creating a new message that synthesises them all.
br_scenario_5: Iterating on drafts — regenerating a creative response multiple times, keeping every version as a branch, and continuing from the one you like best.
sr_seo_desc: Web search built into every chat. Quick lookups are free. Pro unlocks three complexity tiers including a full research pipeline that plans queries, iterates on sources, and writes cited papers.
sr_step1_desc: On iOS, tap the globe icon in the chat toolbar to toggle search on or off; long-press the globe to open the search mode panel and choose a tier (Pro). On web, click the globe to open the panel directly. The globe icon changes colour to show which mode is active — green for basic, blue for web search, orange for research paper.
sr_step2_desc: Pick from three tiers: Basic (single web lookup via Exa, free), Web Search (multi-query with Perplexity Sonar, Pro), or Research Paper (multi-phase pipeline, Pro). Web Search and Research Paper each offer three complexity levels — Quick, Thorough, and Comprehensive — with cost estimates shown in the panel.
sr_step3_desc: Type naturally — Edge handles the rest. For basic searches, it finds and summarises relevant results. For research paper mode, it breaks your question into sub-queries, searches each one, and synthesises findings.
sr_step4_desc: Every answer comes with linked sources. In research paper mode, you see the pipeline running in real time — planning, searching, analysing, and composing — with full citation tracking and a bibliography.
sr_cap_basic_desc: A single web search via Exa built into any chat. Get fast answers with source links without leaving your conversation. No Pro subscription required.
sr_cap_websearch_desc: Powered by Perplexity Sonar. Multi-query search with source analysis across three complexity levels — Quick, Thorough, and Comprehensive.
sr_cap_paper_desc: A multi-phase pipeline that plans queries, iterates on sources, writes sections, and produces a cited paper with bibliography. Three complexity levels available.
sr_cap_complexity_desc: Within each Pro tier, choose Quick, Thorough, or Comprehensive. Cost estimates are shown in the panel so you know what you're spending before you search.
sr_cap_globe_desc: The globe icon in the toolbar changes colour based on your active search mode — green for basic, blue for web search, orange for research paper — so you always know what's enabled.
sr_cap_platform_desc: On iOS, tap to toggle and long-press for the full panel. On web, click to open the panel. On Mac, right-click for a context menu. You can also set a default search tier in Settings.
sr_cap_pipeline_desc: See every step of the research process — planning queries, searching, analysing results, and composing — as it happens in real time.
sr_cap_kb_desc: Combine web search with your uploaded documents. Edge can cross-reference internet sources with your private files.
sr_cap_schedule_desc: Pair search with automated tasks to run recurring research jobs — daily market scans, weekly competitor analysis, and more.
sr_cap_model_desc: Run search with any model. Combine with a persona to get research in a specific voice or from a specific angle.
sr_cap_default_desc: Set your preferred search tier in Settings under Chat Defaults. On iOS, tapping the globe toggles this default on and off so you don't have to pick a tier every time.
sr_scenario_1: Quickly checking a fact during a conversation — asking "what's the current population of Tokyo?" without breaking your flow.
sr_scenario_2: Researching a complex topic — getting a structured, cited report on a market opportunity, technology, or academic question.
sr_scenario_3: Staying current — setting up a recurring research job that scans for news on topics you care about and sends you a daily briefing.
sr_scenario_4: Writing with confidence — verifying claims and finding supporting sources before publishing an article or report.
sr_scenario_5: Comparing information across sources — letting Edge find and synthesize perspectives from multiple websites in one response.
pe_seo_desc: Build specialised AI identities with custom names, avatars, system prompts, models, and tool access. Assign personas to chats, scheduled tasks, or multi-model comparisons.
pe_step1_desc: Give your persona a name, avatar (emoji or image), and a system prompt that defines its personality, expertise, and behaviour.
pe_step2_desc: Choose which AI model the persona uses, set its temperature and token limits, and decide which tools (search, integrations, knowledge base) it can access.
pe_step3_desc: Assign a persona to a chat, a scheduled task, or a multi-model comparison. The persona's personality and settings follow it everywhere.
pe_cap_avatar_desc: Give each persona a unique identity — a name, emoji, or uploaded image. They show up in chat with their own avatar.
pe_cap_prompt_desc: Define exactly how the persona should behave — its tone, expertise, constraints, and priorities. Full control over the AI's personality.
pe_cap_model_desc: Each persona can use a different model and temperature. Your code reviewer runs at low temp for precision; your creative writer runs at high temp for imagination.
pe_cap_tools_desc: Grant or restrict access to search, integrations, and knowledge base per persona. Your research assistant gets web search; your writing coach doesn't.
pe_cap_compare_desc: Use the same persona across multiple models to see how different AIs interpret the same personality and instructions.
pe_cap_tasks_desc: Each step of an automated pipeline can use a different persona. Chain specialised AI identities together for complex workflows.
pe_cap_memory_desc: Memories are available across personas, so each one knows your preferences and context while maintaining its own personality.
pe_scenario_1: A code reviewer persona that uses a low temperature, has a security-focused system prompt, and only produces structured feedback in your preferred style.
pe_scenario_2: A writing coach that encourages creative exploration, suggests vocabulary improvements, and follows your chosen style guide.
pe_scenario_3: A research analyst that has web search enabled, access to your knowledge base, and produces structured reports with citations.
pe_scenario_4: A meeting assistant with calendar integration that can check availability, draft agendas, and summarise meeting notes.
me_seo_desc: AI that remembers you across every conversation. 10 memory categories, 3 retrieval modes, document import, pending review queue, persona scoping, automatic expiry, and full transparency.
me_step1_title: AI extracts facts as you chat
me_step1_desc: After every AI response, Edge analyses the exchange and extracts up to 4 atomic facts worth remembering — preferences, project context, writing style, relationships, goals, and more. Facts are deduplicated and quality-filtered before being offered for saving.
me_step2_desc: In "Ask First" mode, new facts queue as pending memories for you to review. Approve, edit, or discard each one individually — or bulk-approve the entire queue. In automatic mode, facts are saved immediately. You can disable extraction entirely.
me_step3_title: Memories are injected into every conversation
me_step3_desc: Up to 10 always-on memories are injected into every chat. Up to 12 additional memories are retrieved via semantic search — finding facts most relevant to your current conversation. Together they appear in three sections of the system prompt.
me_step4_desc: When new information reinforces an existing memory, the confidence score goes up. When it contradicts one, the old memory is superseded and the new one takes its place. Temporary memories expire automatically — work context after 90 days, transient facts after 21 days.
me_cap_categories_desc: Every memory is filed under one of 10 categories: identity, writing style, work, goals, background, relationships, preferences, tools, skills, and logistics. Browse and filter by category in the Memories view.
me_cap_retrieval_desc: Choose how each memory is used: Always On (injected into every chat, up to 10), Contextual (retrieved via semantic search when relevant — the default), or Disabled (stored but never surfaced).
me_cap_import_desc: Upload PDFs, Word documents, Markdown, or plain text files. Edge extracts memory candidates from the document, then lets you review and commit each one before they're saved.
me_cap_pending_desc: In "Ask First" mode, all new extractions queue as pending memories. Edit any field, approve individually, or bulk approve/reject the entire queue. Nothing is committed until you confirm.
me_cap_extraction_desc: Choose which AI model runs the memory extraction step. Use any model available in your account — useful if you want a cheaper model for extraction and a more powerful one for chat.
me_cap_pin_desc: Pin any memory to boost its retrieval priority. Pinned memories get a +0.35 boost in the ranking algorithm and are more likely to be included in the contextual batch.
me_cap_persona_desc: Memories can be scoped to all personas or only specific ones. A memory about your coding style can be restricted to your developer persona and excluded from your creative writing persona.
me_cap_expiry_desc: Work context memories expire after 90 days. Transient facts expire after 21 days. A daily consolidation job at 3:00 UTC cleans up expired memories and merges reinforced duplicates.
me_cap_reinforce_desc: When new information matches an existing memory, confidence scores increase. When it conflicts, the old memory expires and links to the new one — the full chain is preserved for transparency.
me_cap_semantic_desc: Contextual memories are retrieved using vector embeddings (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small). The system finds the most relevant facts for your current conversation, not just keyword matches.
me_cap_sections_desc: Retrieved memories are injected as three named sections: Response Preferences (how you like answers formatted), User Profile (who you are), and Relevant Context (what's relevant right now).
me_cap_transparent_desc: Every memory is visible, editable, and deletable. See when it was saved, which conversation it came from, how many times it's been used, and its confidence score.
me_cap_control_desc: Edit or delete any memory at any time. Clear all memories with one action. Turn off extraction, turn off injection, or turn off memories entirely — each is a separate setting.
me_cap_pro_desc: Memories are a Pro feature. If your Pro subscription lapses, all memories and their embeddings are purged automatically. No data lingers after downgrade.
me_scenario_1: Never re-explaining your tech stack — the AI remembers your languages, frameworks, and conventions and tailors suggestions accordingly.
me_scenario_2: Maintaining writing consistency — the AI remembers your preferred tone, style, and vocabulary across every writing task, scoped to your writing persona.
me_scenario_3: Project context that persists — your AI knows you're working on a specific project, its goals, and its current status without you having to re-brief it each session.
me_scenario_4: Importing your CV — uploading a resume as a document, reviewing the extracted memory candidates, and letting the AI know your background once for all future conversations.
me_scenario_5: Ask First workflow — reviewing each week's accumulated memory queue in one session: approving the useful facts, editing the ones that need tweaking, and discarding the rest.
me_scenario_6: Persona-scoped knowledge — keeping your work memories in your professional persona and your personal preferences in a separate personal persona, with no cross-contamination.
kb_seo_desc: Upload PDFs, Word documents, and spreadsheets to your knowledge base. Attach them to any conversation, persona, or scheduled task so your AI always has the context it needs.
kb_step1_desc: Drag and drop PDFs, Word documents, spreadsheets, or text files into your knowledge base. They're processed and stored securely.
kb_step2_desc: When starting a chat or configuring a persona, attach one or more knowledge base documents. The AI can reference them as context.
kb_step3_desc: Ask the AI about your uploaded documents. It can find specific information, summarise sections, compare documents, and answer questions grounded in your data.
kb_cap_upload_desc: Support for PDF, DOCX, XLSX, and TXT files. Upload once and reference from any conversation or task.
kb_cap_attach_desc: Pin documents to specific conversations. The AI has access to their content as additional context for every message.
kb_cap_crossref_desc: Ask the AI to compare information across multiple uploaded files. Find inconsistencies, gaps, or patterns.
kb_cap_search_desc: Reference your knowledge base alongside web search results. Ground external research in your private documents.
kb_cap_persona_desc: Control which personas can access your knowledge base. A project manager persona sees project docs; a code reviewer sees technical specs.
kb_cap_tasks_desc: Automated pipelines can reference your knowledge base. Weekly reports can pull from uploaded data sources.
kb_cap_manage_desc: View, rename, and remove documents from your knowledge base at any time. See which chats and tasks reference each file.
kb_cap_available_desc: Your knowledge base syncs across all your devices. Upload on your phone, reference on your desktop.
kb_scenario_1: Uploading a product requirements document and asking the AI to summarise key features, identify gaps, or suggest improvements.
kb_scenario_2: Attaching company guidelines to a writing persona so every response follows your organisation's style and tone.
kb_scenario_3: Uploading financial reports and asking questions like "What was our Q3 revenue?" or "Compare Q2 and Q3 margins."
kb_scenario_4: Building a scheduled task that references your knowledge base to produce weekly status reports grounded in actual project documentation.
is_seo_desc: Transform any AI conversation into a spatial canvas. Messages become draggable nodes connected by their branching structure. Select context nodes, create new branches, and see your thinking spatially.
is_step1_desc: Any conversation can become an Ideascape. Tap the toggle in the chat header and your messages transform into movable nodes on a spatial canvas. Switch back to chat view at any time.
is_step2_desc: Each message appears as a card connected to its parent. Connectors (curved lines) show the conversation's branching structure automatically — forks, merges, and parallel threads are all visible at a glance.
is_step3_desc: Click a node to set it as your focus (the active branch). Use the + button on a card, or Shift/Ctrl+Click, to add nodes to your context — selected nodes get an accent border and a "Context" badge. Then send a new message to create a branch that draws on all selected context.
is_step4_title: Pick up where you left off
is_step4_desc: Node positions persist automatically. Come back days later and your Ideascape is exactly where you left it — your spatial thinking, preserved.
is_cap_drag_desc: Every message becomes a movable card. Drag nodes anywhere on the canvas — group related ideas together, create clusters, or spread them out. Resize cards on desktop.
is_cap_zoom_desc: Zoom in to read details or zoom out to see the full map. Pinch, scroll, or use controls — the canvas grows with your ideas.
is_cap_context_desc: Select one or more nodes as context (+ button or Shift+Click), then send a new message. The AI responds with full awareness of all selected nodes, creating a new branch that merges multiple threads.
is_cap_multimodel_desc: When you use multi-model chat, each model's response becomes its own node — perfect for comparing ideas spatially and branching off the best answer.
is_cap_focus_desc: Click a node to set focus (orange border) — this becomes the active branch. Shift/Ctrl+Click to add to selection (accent border with "Context" badge) — these nodes provide context for your next message.
is_cap_persistent_desc: Node positions save automatically to the server. Your spatial arrangement is always waiting for you, across devices.
is_cap_chat_desc: Add new messages directly from the Ideascape view. The canvas grows as your conversation evolves — new nodes appear connected to their context.
is_cap_toggle_desc: Switch between normal chat view and Ideascape at any time. In chat view, branch points show as interactive pills that let you navigate between sibling branches.
is_cap_help_desc: New to Ideascapes? A built-in help deck explains three key concepts: focus first, add explicit context, and move without losing structure.
is_scenario_1: Brainstorming a project — asking an AI for ideas, arranging them spatially, then selecting the most promising nodes as context for deeper exploration.
is_scenario_2: Comparing multiple AI perspectives — using multi-model chat and spreading out each model's response to compare visually, then branching off the best answer.
is_scenario_3: Planning a complex document — mapping out sections, arguments, and supporting points as a visual tree before you start writing.
is_scenario_4: Learning a new topic — arranging explanations, examples, and follow-up questions into a visual knowledge map. Select earlier explanations as context when asking deeper questions.
is_scenario_5: Creative exploration — generating story ideas, selecting character nodes as context, and asking the AI to weave them into plot points.
at_seo_desc: Create multi-step AI pipelines with their own models, personas, search, and integrations — then schedule them to run automatically. Daily briefings, weekly reports, and background research, hands-free.
at_step1_desc: Define what you want your AI to do — write a briefing, research a topic, summarise emails, or anything else. Give it a name and a description.
at_step2_desc: Add one or more steps to the pipeline. Each step gets its own model, persona, search mode, and integration access. Output from one step feeds into the next.
at_step3_desc: Choose when and how often the task runs — daily, weekly, specific days, or on demand. Edge handles the rest.
at_step4_desc: When a task completes, its output appears in your task history. Review results, re-run manually, or tweak the pipeline based on what you see.
at_cap_pipelines_desc: Chain multiple AI operations together. Each step can use a different model, persona, and configuration.
at_cap_scheduling_desc: Run daily, weekly, on specific days, or on demand. Set the exact time and timezone for each task.
at_cap_personas_desc: Assign a different persona to each pipeline step. A researcher in step one, a writer in step two, an editor in step three.
at_cap_search_desc: Enable web search on any step. The AI can research the web as part of its pipeline execution.
at_cap_integrations_desc: Steps can read emails, check calendars, browse files, and search Notion — all within the pipeline.
at_cap_manual_desc: Trigger any task on demand without waiting for its schedule. Great for testing or one-off needs.
at_cap_history_desc: Every run is logged with its output, duration, and status. Review past results and track how your tasks perform over time.
at_cap_refinement_desc: Tweak pipeline steps, change models, or adjust prompts based on results. Improve your automation over time.
at_scenario_1: Morning briefings — a daily task that searches for industry news, summarises it through a writer persona, and delivers a concise briefing.
at_scenario_2: Weekly reports — automatically gathering data from integrations, analysing trends, and producing a formatted weekly summary.
at_scenario_3: Content monitoring — tracking competitor announcements, product launches, or market changes on a daily schedule.
at_scenario_4: Research automation — running deep research queries on a schedule and building up a library of findings over time.
at_scenario_5: Email digests — summarising the week's emails every Friday afternoon so nothing falls through the cracks.
ig_seo_desc: Connect Google, Microsoft, Notion, and Apple Calendar to NanthAI Edge. Read emails, browse files, manage calendars, and search Notion — all from within your AI conversations.
ig_step1_desc: Go to Settings → Integrations and tap Connect for Google, Microsoft, Notion, or Apple Calendar. You'll authorise through the provider's standard OAuth flow.
ig_step2_desc: Once connected, your AI can read emails, browse files, check calendars, and search Notion — right from any conversation. Just ask.
ig_step3_desc: Give specific personas or scheduled tasks access to your integrations. Your code reviewer persona can browse your repo docs; your assistant can check your calendar.
ig_cap_email_desc: Access your Gmail or Outlook inbox. Search, read, summarise, draft replies, and send — all from within a chat.
ig_cap_files_desc: Search and read documents from Google Drive or OneDrive. Your AI can reference your files without you needing to upload them.
ig_cap_calendar_desc: View upcoming events, find free slots, create new events, and manage your schedule across Google Calendar, Outlook, or Apple Calendar.
ig_cap_notion_desc: Search and read Notion pages and databases. Cross-reference your team wiki, project docs, or personal notes in any chat.
ig_cap_search_desc: Combine integrations with web search. Your AI can cross-reference internal documents with external research in the same conversation.
ig_cap_tasks_desc: Automated tasks can access integrations. Build pipelines that check emails, browse files, and update calendars on a schedule.
ig_cap_oauth_desc: All connections use standard OAuth. Your credentials are never stored in Edge — only secure tokens that you can revoke at any time.
ig_google_coming_soon_title: Google Workspace is coming soon on web while Google scope approval is pending.
ig_google_coming_soon_body: Microsoft 365, Notion, and Apple Calendar are available now.
ig_scenario_1: Asking your AI to summarise this morning's emails and flag anything urgent — without leaving your chat.
ig_scenario_2: Researching a topic and having the AI cross-reference your Notion wiki with web search results.
ig_scenario_3: Checking your calendar for free slots and drafting a meeting invite — all in one conversation.
ig_scenario_4: Building a scheduled task that reads your emails every morning and writes a daily digest using your writer persona.
cd_seo_desc: Set your ideal AI starting point. Choose default models, temperature, and token limits. Pin favourite model combos and personas for one-tap access from the chat list.
cd_step1_desc: Open Settings and choose the model, temperature, and max token limit that every new conversation starts with. No more re-configuring each time.
cd_step2_desc: Save model combos, personas, or multi-model configurations to the favourites strip. They appear as circular avatars at the top of your chat list for instant access.
cd_step3_desc: Tap any favourite to start a new conversation pre-configured with that model, persona, or multi-model setup. Skip the setup entirely.
cd_cap_default_model_desc: Choose which AI model new conversations use by default from any of the 150+ available models. Pick once, and every new chat starts ready.
cd_cap_temp_desc: Set your preferred creativity level. Lower for precise, factual answers; higher for creative, exploratory responses.
cd_cap_tokens_desc: Control maximum response length. Set it once and forget it — every conversation respects your preference.
cd_cap_favstrip_desc: A scrollable row of your go-to configurations. Single models show their provider logo; multi-model combos show overlapping avatars.
cd_cap_multifav_desc: Save your favourite 2- or 3-model combinations. One tap starts a multi-model chat with exactly the models you want.
cd_cap_personafav_desc: Pin personas alongside models. Start a conversation that's already configured with the right AI personality and settings.
cd_cap_override_desc: Defaults are starting points, not limits. Change the model or settings for any individual conversation at any time.
cd_scenario_1: Coding sessions — setting a low-temperature default so every new chat is optimised for precise, deterministic code generation.
cd_scenario_2: Creative writing — saving a high-temperature favourite for when you want exploratory, imaginative responses.
cd_scenario_3: Quick comparisons — pinning a 3-model combo so you can instantly launch a multi-model chat without picking models each time.
cd_scenario_4: Research workflows — starting every new conversation with your preferred research persona, search enabled, ready to go.
fo_seo_desc: Organise AI conversations into named, coloured folders. Move chats in bulk, filter the sidebar by folder, and search across all conversations by title or content.
fo_step1_desc: Tap "New Folder" in the sidebar. Give it a name and pick a colour. Folders appear at the top of your conversation list.
fo_step2_desc: Long-press any chat (or use the action menu) and move it to a folder. You can also select multiple chats and move them in bulk.
fo_step3_desc: Tap a folder to see only its conversations. Use the search bar to find chats by title or content — across all folders or within one.
fo_cap_named_desc: Create as many folders as you need. Each gets a custom name and colour so you can visually distinguish your work, personal, and project chats.
fo_cap_colours_desc: Pick from a range of colours for each folder. The colour shows in the sidebar and on the folder badges throughout the app.
fo_cap_bulk_desc: Select multiple conversations and move them all to a folder at once. Reorganise quickly without tedious one-by-one moves.
fo_cap_filter_desc: Tap a folder to instantly filter the conversation list. See only what's relevant right now.
fo_cap_search_desc: Full-text search works across all folders. Find any conversation by title or content, no matter where it's filed.
fo_cap_sync_desc: Folder assignments sync across all your devices. Organise on your phone and see the same structure on your tablet or desktop.
fo_cap_unfiled_desc: Conversations that aren't in any folder appear in the "Unfiled" section — nothing gets lost, everything has a place.
fo_cap_scheduled_desc: When an automated task creates a conversation, it lands in the system-generated "Scheduled" folder automatically. It sorts to the bottom and stays out of your way on mobile.
fo_scenario_1: Keeping work and personal chats separate — creating a "Work" folder for project discussions and a "Personal" folder for everything else.
fo_scenario_2: Managing research projects — dedicating a folder to each project so you can instantly find related conversations.
fo_scenario_3: Cleaning up after a busy week — selecting all the chats you no longer need and moving them to an "Archive" folder.
fo_scenario_4: Onboarding to a new topic — searching across all folders to find previous conversations about the same subject.
th_seo_desc: Customise NanthAI Edge with dark mode, light mode, or system-aware switching. Choose from four accent colour themes that sync across all your devices.
th_step1_desc: Tap your profile or head to Settings. The Appearance section is right at the top — no digging required.
th_step2_desc: Pick Light, Dark, or System. System follows your device setting automatically, so Edge always matches the rest of your phone.
th_step3_desc: Choose from four colour palettes — Vibrant, High Contrast, Teal, or Lilac. Each one tints buttons, badges, and highlights throughout the app.
th_step4_desc: Your appearance preferences sync via your account. Set it once on your iPhone and it applies on your iPad, Mac, and the web — everywhere you use Edge.
th_cap_dark_desc: A true dark theme built on deep blacks and muted surfaces. Easier on your eyes at night and saves battery on OLED screens.
th_cap_light_desc: A clean, bright interface for daytime use. High contrast text on white surfaces for maximum readability.
th_cap_system_desc: Set it to System and Edge follows your device's light/dark schedule. Switch modes automatically at sunset and sunrise.
th_cap_accents_desc: Vibrant (teal + coral + amber), High Contrast, Teal, and Lilac. Each theme tints interactive elements, badges, and highlights.
th_cap_a11y_desc: All themes meet WCAG contrast requirements. Text remains crisp and readable regardless of which palette you choose.
th_cap_sync_desc: Your theme choice syncs to every device. Set it on iPhone, see it on iPad, Mac, and the web — no need to configure twice.
th_scenario_1: Late-night brainstorming sessions where dark mode protects your eyes and keeps the focus on ideas.
th_scenario_2: Matching your phone's aesthetic — pick an accent that complements your wallpaper and system theme.
th_scenario_3: High-contrast mode for bright environments — airports, outdoors, conference rooms with overhead lights.
th_scenario_4: Making Edge feel like yours — the same AI, styled the way you prefer.
pt_seo_desc: NanthAI Edge shows you the cost of every AI response, a live chat total, your OpenRouter balance, and a 4-bucket breakdown (Responses, Memory, Search, Other). Full price transparency — zero markup.
pt_step1_desc: In Settings → OpenRouter, toggle 'Show Advanced Stats'. That's the only step — cost data starts appearing immediately in every chat.
pt_step2_title: See the cost of every response
pt_step2_desc: Each assistant message shows its generation cost next to the timestamp — e.g. '3:45 PM · $0.0023'. The figure reflects only that response's tokens, not background tasks.
pt_step3_desc: The header subtitle updates in real time to show the full session cost: 'GPT-4o · $0.0158'. Tap it to open the cost breakdown.
pt_step4_desc: The popover splits costs into four buckets: Responses, Memory, Search, and Other. You see exactly which features are using your credits.
pt_cap_permsg_desc: Every assistant message shows its individual generation cost — 4 decimal places, right next to the timestamp. No guessing, no rounding.
pt_cap_total_desc: The chat header shows a running total that updates after every response. Tap it to open the full cost breakdown without leaving the conversation.
pt_cap_buckets_desc: Costs split into Responses (your AI replies), Memory (extraction and retrieval), Search (web and paper queries), and Other (title generation, compaction). Know where every cent goes.
pt_cap_balance_desc: Your OpenRouter credit balance appears right in the chat header. Low? You'll see it before you run out — no surprise interruptions.
pt_cap_multimodel_desc: In multi-model chats, each model's response shows its individual cost. A group total appears at the bottom so you can compare model efficiency at a glance.
pt_cap_nomarkup_desc: Every cost figure comes straight from OpenRouter. NanthAI adds no markup. The number you see is the number OpenRouter charged — nothing more.
pt_cap_streaming_desc: Costs appear as '$—' while a response is streaming and resolve to a real figure the moment the model finishes. No stale placeholders.
pt_cap_toggle_desc: Prefer a cleaner UI? Turn Advanced Stats off and cost figures disappear. The feature is opt-in and non-intrusive — you're always in control.
pt_scenario_1: Developers running experiments who want to know exactly how much each model call costs.
pt_scenario_2: Cost-conscious users who want to stay on budget and avoid surprise OpenRouter charges.
pt_scenario_3: Teams using Memory and Search features who want to understand the full cost of each conversation.
pt_scenario_4: Power users comparing model efficiency — same output, different price per token.
pt_bucket_responses_desc: The primary generation cost — every token your AI model produces as a reply. This is the number shown per-message.
pt_bucket_memory_desc: Background calls for memory extraction (finding facts to save), embedding (storing them for retrieval), and similarity search (finding relevant memories before each reply).
pt_bucket_search_desc: Web and academic paper searches: query generation, web search calls, research planning, source analysis, and synthesis steps in a full research pipeline.
pt_bucket_other_desc: Housekeeping tasks: automatic title generation, context compaction when a conversation gets very long, and subagent orchestration calls.
bk_seo_desc: NanthAI Edge uses a bring-your-own-key model via OpenRouter. Pay only for the tokens you use at listed rates — zero markup. Add your own AWS, Google, or Azure keys for direct rate control.
bk_step1_desc: Head to openrouter.ai and sign up. Add credits to your account — you can start with as little as $5.
bk_step2_desc: In Edge, go to Settings and tap 'Connect OpenRouter'. A secure OAuth flow links your account — no API key copy-pasting required.
bk_step3_title: Start chatting — pay only for tokens
bk_step3_desc: Every message is billed at OpenRouter's listed rate for the model you choose. NanthAI adds zero markup. You see the exact cost per message.
bk_step4_desc: For even more control, add your own AWS Bedrock, Google AI, or Azure OpenAI keys to OpenRouter. When you use a model hosted by that provider, your key is used and you pay your negotiated rate.
bk_cap_zero_desc: NanthAI doesn't add any fee on top of OpenRouter's model pricing. Every token costs exactly what OpenRouter lists — nothing more.
bk_cap_oauth_desc: Connect via a standard OAuth flow using PKCE. Your credentials never touch NanthAI's servers. Revoke access at any time from OpenRouter.
bk_cap_provider_desc: Add your AWS Bedrock, Google AI, or Azure OpenAI keys on OpenRouter. When Edge uses a model from that provider, your key is honoured and billed at your direct rate.
bk_cap_models_desc: Your OpenRouter account gives you access to ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, and hundreds more — all through a single connection.
bk_cap_cost_desc: See your OpenRouter credit balance directly in Edge. Get low-balance alerts before you run out so you're never interrupted mid-conversation.
bk_cap_control_desc: Set spending limits on OpenRouter. Choose cheaper models for casual chats and premium models for important tasks. You're always in control.
bk_cap_dashboard_desc: View detailed token-level usage, cost breakdowns by model, and billing history directly on openrouter.ai. Complete transparency.
bk_cap_disconnect_desc: Disconnect your OpenRouter account from Edge whenever you want. Your conversations stay intact — only the API connection is removed.
bk_scenario_1: Developers who already have an OpenRouter account and want to use it with a polished AI chat interface.
bk_scenario_2: Cost-conscious users who want transparent, per-token pricing with no hidden fees or subscriptions.
bk_scenario_3: Enterprise teams who need to route AI traffic through their own AWS Bedrock or Azure accounts for compliance.
bk_scenario_4: Power users who switch between models frequently and want a single billing dashboard for all of them.
bk_explainer_intro: OpenRouter supports a Bring Your Own Key model. When you add a provider API key (AWS Bedrock, Google AI, Azure OpenAI) to your OpenRouter account, any request to a model hosted by that provider uses your key instead of OpenRouter's default routing. This means:
bk_explainer_bullet1: You pay your negotiated rate with the cloud provider — often lower than list price for enterprise accounts.
bk_explainer_bullet2: Traffic routes through your cloud account, which may be required for data residency or compliance.
bk_explainer_bullet3: You keep the same seamless Edge experience — no configuration changes needed in the app.
pf_seo_desc: Compare NanthAI Edge Free and Pro tiers. Free includes multi-model chat, folders, themes, and BYOK. Pro is a one-time $4.99 purchase that unlocks personas, memory, scheduled tasks, integrations, and more.
pf_step1_title: Start free — no credit card needed
pf_step1_desc: Create an account and start chatting immediately. Multi-model chat, folders, themes, basic web search, and BYOK are all included at no cost.
pf_step2_title: Use it as long as you like
pf_step2_desc: There's no trial period and no artificial limits on the free tier. You pay only for the AI tokens you use through your OpenRouter account.
pf_step3_desc: When you're ready for personas, memory, scheduled tasks, integrations, and deep research, unlock Pro for a one-time $4.99 payment. No subscriptions.
pf_cap_chat_desc: Send the same prompt to up to 3 AI models at once. Compare responses, fork conversations, and queue follow-ups — all included free.
pf_cap_search_desc: Toggle the globe icon to add real-time web results to any conversation. Quick lookups with no upgrade required.
pf_cap_folders_desc: Create folders, move chats in bulk, search across all conversations. Keep everything tidy from day one.
pf_cap_themes_desc: Dark mode, light mode, system-aware switching, and four accent colour themes — all synced across your devices.
pf_cap_byok_desc: Connect your OpenRouter account and pay only for the tokens you use. No markup from NanthAI. Add your own provider keys for even more control.
pf_cap_personas_desc: Create AI identities with custom names, avatars, system prompts, model assignments, and per-persona tool access.
pf_cap_memories_desc: AI that remembers your preferences, projects, and writing style across every conversation. Fully transparent and under your control.
pf_cap_tasks_desc: Create recurring AI jobs — daily briefings, weekly summaries, monitoring pipelines — each with its own model, persona, and tools.
pf_cap_integrations_desc: Connect Google, Microsoft, Notion, and Apple Calendar. Read emails, browse files, manage events, and search Notion — all from within a chat.
pf_cap_kb_desc: Upload PDFs, docs, and spreadsheets. Attach them to any chat or scheduled job so the AI always has the context it needs.
pf_cap_research_desc: Three research tiers beyond basic search — including a full pipeline that plans queries, iterates on sources, and generates a cited paper.
pf_cap_ideascapes_desc: Switch any conversation into a spatial canvas. Arrange AI responses as movable nodes and explore ideas visually.
pf_scenario_1: Casual users who want a powerful multi-model AI chat without paying anything up front.
pf_scenario_2: Developers who want to bring their own API key and only pay for actual token usage — no platform markup.
pf_scenario_3: Power users who want the full toolkit — personas, memory, scheduled tasks, integrations — for a single $4.99 purchase.
pf_scenario_4: Teams evaluating AI tools — start free, upgrade individuals as needed, no ongoing subscription to manage.
lic_seo_desc: NanthAI Edge source code licensing. Free for personal self-hosting. Commercial use requires a paid license.
lic_hero_desc: NanthAI Edge is source-available. The source code is free to use for personal self-hosting and learning. Businesses and organisations that want to deploy, rebrand, or build on the codebase need a Commercial License.
lic_note_title: Looking to use NanthAI Edge as a product?
lic_note_body: You don't need a source code license to use NanthAI Edge. Just sign up, connect your OpenRouter key, and go. Pro features are unlocked with a one-time purchase. This page is specifically about licensing the source code for self-hosting or commercial use.
lic_s1_body: The NanthAI Edge source code is available under a source-available license that permits personal use, self-hosting, evaluation, and learning — but not commercial use. The full license text is in the LICENSE file in the repository.
lic_s2_body: Commercial Use means deploying, modifying, or building on the source code in a business context. This includes:
lic_s2_item1: Deploying a self-hosted instance for your company or team
lic_s2_item2: Rebranding or white-labelling the codebase as your own product
lic_s2_item3: Using the source code as part of a product, service, or internal tooling
lic_s2_item4: Offering a hosted version to clients or customers
lic_s2_item5: Any use by employees or contractors on behalf of a commercial entity
lic_s3_body: The following uses do NOT require a Commercial License:
lic_s3_item1: Personal self-hosting for your own individual use
lic_s3_item4: Evaluation or proof-of-concept work (up to 30 days)
lic_s4_title: 4. What does a commercial license grant?
lic_s4_body: A Commercial License is negotiated per customer and may include:
lic_s4_item1: Internal business use — deploy for your team or organisation
lic_s4_item2: Rebranding and white-labelling — customise the UI, name, and branding
lic_s4_item3: Modification and extension — build custom features on the platform
lic_s4_item5: Priority support — direct access for technical guidance
lic_s4_item6: Update entitlements — access to new versions for a defined period
lic_s5_item1_desc: perpetual license for a specific version.
lic_s6_q1: Can I try it before buying a license?
lic_s6_a1: Yes. The default license permits evaluation and proof-of-concept use for up to 30 days. If you need more time, let us know.
lic_s6_a2: For personal use: yes, freely. For commercial use: yes, with a Commercial License. Modifications remain subject to the license terms.
lic_s6_q3: Can I redistribute my modified version?
lic_s6_a3: Not without a Commercial License that explicitly permits redistribution. The default license does not allow redistribution.
lic_s6_q4: Is there a free tier for startups?
lic_s6_a4: Contact us. We're open to discounted or deferred licensing for early-stage startups on a case-by-case basis.
